data.title: ATEC2026 Launches as the “Turing Test” for Embodied AI, Challenging Robots to Survive the Real World
data.description: The ATEC2026 – AI and Robotics Real-World Extreme Challenge commenced in Hong Kong with the participation of The Chinese University of Hong Kong and Shanghai Innovation Institute. This event, organized by the Advanced Technology Exploration Community (ATEC), is focused on establishing a framework resembling a "Turing Test" for embodied AI. The goal is to push robots to exhibit stability and intelligence in open, dynamic, and unstructured settings, moving away from controlled laboratory environments. The competition seeks to test the capabilities of AI and robotics outside conventional boundaries, promoting real-world applications.
Anthropic CEO to meet Susie Wiles at White House Friday - Axios

data.title: Nidec Announces the Receipt of the Third-Party Committee’s Investigation Report (Final Report) and Our Company’s Response
data.description: In Kyoto, Japan, an investigation by the Third-Party Committee into instances of improper accounting within the Group continues following the receipt of their investigation report. This update follows the "Notice Regarding the Third-Party Committee’s Investigation Report” on February 27, 2026, and the “Announcement Regarding the Disclosure of the Third-Party Committee’s Investigation Report and Our Company’s Response” on March 3, 2026. The company is actively addressing the findings from the report as part of ongoing efforts to rectify the situation.

data.title: Geviti Launches First-to-Market Free, Clinically Backed AI Platform for Health Data Interpretation
data.description: A company called Geviti has introduced a new AI platform in Phoenix that is supported by clinical research. This platform is capable of transforming intricate blood biomarker information into easily understandable and practical health recommendations. The service is being offered for free to users.

data.title: CoStar Data Shows Aire Park Driving New Rental Highs in Leeds
data.description: London - Demand for office space in Leeds is moving south of the river to the East Quarter, based on data from CoStar. The City Core, traditionally the main office location in Leeds, experienced a drop in its share of leasing activity from a five-year average of 80% to 60% in 2025. This shift is attributed to a rising interest from professional and financial services companies in the East Quarter. CoStar, a prominent provider of online real estate services and analytics, highlighted this trend in the property market.

data.title: Fortuna Renews Share Buyback Program
data.description: Fortuna Mining Corp., listed on TSX as FVI and NYSE as FSM, based in Vancouver, British Columbia, disclosed on April 17, 2026, that their Board of Directors has given the green light for the extension of their regular course issuer bid (NCIB). The bid allows for the acquisition of up to five percent of the company's common shares that were outstanding as of April 10, 2026.
